title: What is a Skip Connection?
concept: A skip connection lets a CNN block send its input forward along a shortcut and combine it later, so useful features do not have to be rebuilt at every layer.

exercises.prompt: Recap: If early CNN clues may still matter later, what architectural idea would keep those earlier features directly available deeper in the network?

options.text: A shortcut that carries the earlier activation forward
Deleting early features as soon as deeper layers appear
Saving the image as a larger file

exercises.explain: Exactly — that direct shortcut idea is the starting intuition behind skip connections.

cards.title: Why keep a bridge?
cards.body: Imagine a team tracing a city map. If each person must redraw the entire map from the previous person's version, small distortions can pile up. Keeping a clean bridge back to the earlier sketch makes later edits safer. Skip connections give deep CNN blocks that kind of bridge.

cards.title: A shortcut for activations
cards.body: A skip connection lets a block send its input along a shortcut path while the main path still applies normal learned layers. The two paths are combined later, often by addition. That means the block can preserve useful earlier features while also learning something new.

cards.title: An express elevator
cards.body: In a tall building, some people take an express elevator instead of stopping at every floor. The local floors still matter, but not every passenger needs every stop. A skip connection gives information that kind of express route through part of the network.

cards.title: Carry the ear edges forward
cards.body: Suppose early CNN layers already found crisp dog-ear edges. A later block may want to combine those with fur texture or head-shape clues. With a skip connection, the later block can still access the earlier ear-edge signal instead of hoping every layer in between preserved it perfectly.

cards.title: Skipping is not the same as 'do nothing'
cards.body: A common myth is that skip connections make the middle layers useless. Not true. The main path still learns important transformations. The skip path just keeps a reliable copy of the earlier signal available, so the block can preserve and refine at the same time.

left.text:
Every feature must survive
every learned layer in order.
No direct shortcut exists.

right.text:
Input also travels a shortcut.
Main path can add refinements later.
Useful base signal stays reachable.

exercises.prompt: Which description best matches a skip connection?

options.text: A shortcut that carries a block's input forward so it can be combined later
A rule that deletes the block input before deeper layers see it
A pooling layer that always shrinks the image in half
A file-format trick for saving feature maps

exercises.explain: Right — the key idea is a direct route for the block input, not forced layer-by-layer rewriting only.

exercises.prompt: True or false: If a CNN uses a skip connection, the deeper layers inside that block can no longer learn anything useful.
exercises.explain: False. The deeper layers still learn. The skip path simply means they do not have to carry the whole burden alone.

exercises.sentence: A skip connection lets information ___ around part of the network and join later.
exercises.explain: Jump is right — the signal takes a shortcut route around some learned layers.

exercises.prompt: Which setup sounds most like a skip connection inside a CNN block?

options.text: The block transforms the input, but also carries the original input forward and combines it at the end
The block replaces all feature maps with a random new image
The block stores the label outside the network and never uses activations

exercises.explain: Exactly — skip connections are about routing activations, not deleting or randomizing them.

exercises.prompt: An early CNN block already found clean wheel edges in a bike photo. A later block wants to sharpen and combine those features without risking their loss. Which design is the best fit?

options.text: Use a skip connection so the original wheel-edge features can travel forward while the later block learns refinements
Force every later layer to rebuild the wheel edges from scratch with no shortcut
Delete the early feature map before the later block begins learning
Replace the convolution block with a file-compression step

exercises.explain: Exactly. A skip connection keeps the earlier wheel-edge signal available while later layers focus on improving it. Next, we'll see the classic block that uses this idea over and over: the residual block.
